name: Marketing Basics
description: An introduction to core marketing concepts including the marketing mix, consumer behavior, market research, digital marketing, branding, and building a complete marketing plan.

modules.title: What Is Marketing?
modules.objectives: Define marketing and explain its role in business; understand the marketing mix (4Ps); distinguish marketing from selling; describe how marketing creates value for customers and firms.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Marketing Ch 1 — Marketing and the Marketing Process
modules.video2Note: YouTube — What Is Marketing? The 4Ps Explained
modules.assignment: Find a real-world advertisement and identify how it uses each of the 4Ps (Product, Price, Place, Promotion); write a paragraph explaining the value the product offers to its target customer.

modules.title: Marketing Environment & Research
modules.objectives: Distinguish the micro and macro marketing environments; describe how PESTLE factors affect marketing decisions; explain the steps in the marketing research process and the types of market data.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Marketing Ch 2 — Strategic Planning in Marketing
modules.assignment: Choose a business and conduct a PESTLE analysis identifying at least two factors in each category; explain how two of those factors could affect their marketing strategy.

modules.title: Consumer Behavior
modules.objectives: Identify the steps in the consumer buying decision process; explain psychological, social, cultural, and situational factors that influence purchasing decisions; apply consumer behavior insights to marketing strategy.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Marketing Ch 3 — Consumer Markets and Buying Behavior
modules.assignment: Walk through the five-step consumer decision process for a recent purchase you made; identify which psychological or social factors most influenced each step and how a marketer could influence your decision.

modules.title: Market Segmentation, Targeting & Positioning
modules.titleZh: 市场细分、目标市场与定位
modules.objectives: Define market segmentation and explain the four major segmentation bases; describe target market selection strategies; construct a positioning statement and perceptual map.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Marketing Ch 5 — Market Segmentation, Targeting, and Positioning
modules.video2Note: YouTube — STP Marketing: Segmentation, Targeting, and Positioning
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Segmentation and Targeting Practice
modules.assignment: Choose a consumer product; segment its market using at least three segmentation bases, select a primary target segment, and write a positioning statement for that segment.

modules.title: Product Strategy
modules.objectives: Distinguish between core, actual, and augmented product levels; explain the product life cycle and its marketing implications; understand product mix decisions including width, length, and depth.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Marketing Ch 9 — Products: Consumer and Industrial Goods
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Product Life Cycle: Introduction, Growth, Maturity, Decline
modules.assignment: Select a real product and identify which stage of the product life cycle it is currently in; recommend and justify two specific marketing strategies appropriate for that stage.

modules.title: Pricing Strategy
modules.objectives: Explain internal and external factors affecting pricing decisions; compare pricing strategies including cost-plus, value-based, competitive, penetration, and skimming; understand psychological pricing.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Marketing Ch 11 — Pricing Products and Services
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Pricing Strategies: Penetration vs Price Skimming
modules.assignment: A new coffee shop is opening in your city; recommend and justify a pricing strategy for its signature drinks, explaining why you chose it over at least two alternative approaches.

modules.title: Distribution & Place
modules.objectives: Describe marketing channels and their functions; compare direct and indirect distribution; explain channel conflict and management; understand logistics, supply chain, and omnichannel strategies.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Marketing Ch 12 — Distribution Channels
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Marketing Channels and Distribution Strategy
modules.assignment: Map the distribution channel for a product of your choice from manufacturer to consumer; identify every intermediary and explain the function each one performs.

modules.title: Promotion & Advertising
modules.objectives: Describe the promotional mix elements (advertising, sales promotion, public relations, personal selling, direct marketing); explain the communication process in marketing; compare push and pull strategies.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Marketing Ch 13 — The Promotion Mix
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Advertising vs Public Relations vs Sales Promotion
modules.assignment: Design a simple integrated promotional campaign for a school bake sale; use at least three elements of the promotional mix and explain the role each plays in communicating your message.

quizQuestions.question: The four elements of the marketing mix are known as:
quizQuestions.answer: Product, Price, Place, Promotion
quizQuestions.explanation: The classic 4Ps of marketing — Product, Price, Place, and Promotion — form the foundational marketing mix that companies use to market their offerings.

quizQuestions.question: Marketing differs from selling because marketing:
options: Focuses on identifying and satisfying customer needs profitably
Occurs only after a product is made
quizQuestions.answer: Focuses on identifying and satisfying customer needs profitably
quizQuestions.explanation: While selling pushes existing products to customers, marketing takes a broader approach: researching needs, creating products that satisfy those needs, and building long-term relationships.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The four elements of the marketing mix are known as:" is: Product, Price, Place, Promotion.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Product, Price, Place, Promotion is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "which of the following is an example of a service rather than a physical product."
quizQuestions.answer: A haircut
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: A haircut.

quizQuestions.question: Primary market research data is:
options: Data already published by other organizations
Original data collected directly for a specific research purpose
quizQuestions.answer: Original data collected directly for a specific research purpose
quizQuestions.explanation: Primary research involves gathering new data firsthand through surveys, interviews, focus groups, or observations — customized to the researcher's specific question.

quizQuestions.question: PESTLE analysis examines which external factors?
options: Product, Elasticity, Supply, Technology, Labor, Expenses
Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, Environmental
Price, Employees, Segments, Target, Location, Expansion
Profit, Equity, Sales, Trade, Logistics, Efficiency
quizQuestions.answer: Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, Environmental
quizQuestions.explanation: PESTLE is a framework for analyzing macro-environmental factors: Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, and Environmental.

quizQuestions.question: A focus group in market research involves:
options: A small group discussion moderated to gather qualitative insights
A one-on-one interview with an expert
quizQuestions.answer: A small group discussion moderated to gather qualitative insights
quizQuestions.explanation: Focus groups bring together 6–12 participants to discuss a product, brand, or concept in depth, generating rich qualitative data about attitudes and perceptions.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Primary market research data is:" is: Original data collected directly for a specific research purpose.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Original data collected directly for a specific research purpose is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The first step in the consumer buying decision process is:
quizQuestions.answer: Problem recognition
quizQuestions.explanation: The buying process begins when the consumer recognizes a need or problem — the gap between their current state and desired state that motivates them to seek a solution.

quizQuestions.question: Social factors that influence consumer behavior include:
options: Family, reference groups, and social class
quizQuestions.answer: Family, reference groups, and social class
quizQuestions.explanation: Social factors include the influence of family, peer groups, social media influencers, and social class on consumer attitudes and purchasing decisions.

quizQuestions.question: Brand loyalty is an example of which consumer behavior factor?
quizQuestions.answer: Psychological factor
quizQuestions.explanation: Brand loyalty relates to learning and attitudes — psychological factors that determine how consumers perceive and respond to brands over time.

quizQuestions.question: Demographic segmentation divides markets based on:
quizQuestions.answer: Age, income, gender, and education
quizQuestions.explanation: Demographic segmentation uses measurable population characteristics — age, gender, income, education, occupation, family size — to divide the market.

quizQuestions.question: Psychographic segmentation is based on:
options: Values, interests, lifestyles, and personality traits
quizQuestions.answer: Values, interests, lifestyles, and personality traits
quizQuestions.explanation: Psychographic segmentation groups consumers by psychological characteristics — values, attitudes, interests, and lifestyle choices that influence buying behavior.

quizQuestions.question: A perceptual map is used in marketing to:
options: Show how consumers perceive competing brands on key attributes
quizQuestions.answer: Show how consumers perceive competing brands on key attributes
quizQuestions.explanation: A perceptual map plots brands based on consumer perceptions across two dimensions (e.g., price vs. quality), helping marketers identify competitive positioning gaps.

quizQuestions.question: An undifferentiated marketing strategy targets:
options: Multiple distinct segments with different offers
The entire market with one standard offer
quizQuestions.answer: The entire market with one standard offer
quizQuestions.explanation: An undifferentiated (mass marketing) strategy treats the market as homogeneous and uses one marketing mix for all consumers — efficient but less tailored.

quizQuestions.question: In the product life cycle, sales grow most rapidly during the:
quizQuestions.answer: Growth stage
quizQuestions.explanation: During the growth stage, consumer awareness increases, sales rise rapidly, and new competitors enter the market attracted by profitability.

quizQuestions.question: The core product refers to:
options: The physical features and packaging of a product
The fundamental benefit or problem-solving service the customer buys
quizQuestions.answer: The fundamental benefit or problem-solving service the customer buys
quizQuestions.explanation: The core product is the essential benefit — what the customer is really buying. A drill's core product is the hole, not the drill itself.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "In the product life cycle, sales grow most rapidly during the:" is: Growth stage.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Growth stage is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The core product refers to:" is: The physical features and packaging of a product.
quizQuestions.answer: False
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: The fundamental benefit or problem-solving service the customer buys.

quizQuestions.question: Cost-plus pricing sets the price by:
options: Adding a markup to the total cost of producing the product
Charging what the customer is willing to pay
Setting price below competitors to gain market share
quizQuestions.answer: Adding a markup to the total cost of producing the product
quizQuestions.explanation: Cost-plus pricing = total cost + desired profit margin. It is simple and ensures costs are covered, but may ignore what customers value or what competitors charge.

quizQuestions.question: Price skimming involves:
options: Setting a low initial price to capture market share quickly
Setting a high initial price and lowering it over time
Setting prices the same as competitors
Offering deep discounts to clear inventory
quizQuestions.answer: Setting a high initial price and lowering it over time
quizQuestions.explanation: Price skimming targets early adopters willing to pay a premium, then lowers price as competition increases and the mass market is targeted.

quizQuestions.question: Penetration pricing is best suited for:
options: Products with a high-income target market
Gaining rapid market share in a price-sensitive market
quizQuestions.answer: Gaining rapid market share in a price-sensitive market
quizQuestions.explanation: Penetration pricing sets a low initial price to attract a large customer base quickly — effective in competitive markets with price-sensitive consumers.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Cost-plus pricing sets the price by:" is: Adding a markup to the total cost of producing the product.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Adding a markup to the total cost of producing the product is correct.

quizQuestions.question: An intermediary in a distribution channel:
options: Connects producers to consumers by performing distribution functions
quizQuestions.answer: Connects producers to consumers by performing distribution functions
quizQuestions.explanation: Intermediaries (wholesalers, retailers, agents) add value by performing functions like storage, transportation, promotion, and breaking bulk between producer and consumer.

quizQuestions.question: Direct distribution means the producer:
options: Sells directly to the end consumer without intermediaries
quizQuestions.answer: Sells directly to the end consumer without intermediaries
quizQuestions.explanation: Direct distribution (e.g., a company website or factory store) eliminates intermediaries, giving the producer greater control over customer experience and higher margins.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "An intermediary in a distribution channel:" is: Connects producers to consumers by performing distribution functions.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Connects producers to consumers by performing distribution functions is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Which element of the promotional mix involves mass communication through paid media?
quizQuestions.answer: Advertising
quizQuestions.explanation: Advertising is paid, non-personal mass communication through media such as TV, radio, print, digital, and outdoor — used to reach large audiences.

quizQuestions.question: A push promotional strategy focuses on:
options: Creating consumer demand that pulls products through channels
Persuading intermediaries to stock and promote the product
Using social media to engage customers directly
Building brand loyalty among end users
quizQuestions.answer: Persuading intermediaries to stock and promote the product
quizQuestions.explanation: A push strategy 'pushes' products through the distribution channel using trade promotions, personal selling, and incentives directed at retailers and wholesalers.

quizQuestions.question: Sales promotion includes tactics such as:
options: Coupons, rebates, contests, and buy-one-get-one offers
quizQuestions.answer: Coupons, rebates, contests, and buy-one-get-one offers
quizQuestions.explanation: Sales promotions are short-term incentives designed to stimulate immediate purchasing — including coupons, sweepstakes, samples, and temporary price reductions.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which element of the promotional mix involves mass communication through paid media" is: Advertising.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Advertising is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the 4Ps of the marketing mix?
questions.answer: Profit
questions.explanation: The 4Ps are Product, Price, Place, and Promotion. Profit is an outcome of marketing success, not an element of the marketing mix.

questions.question: Which type of market research involves direct observation of consumers in their natural setting?
questions.answer: Ethnographic research
questions.explanation: Ethnographic research involves researchers observing consumers in their natural environment to understand behavior as it actually occurs — providing deep contextual insights.

questions.question: Post-purchase behavior is important to marketers because:
options: It determines the initial price of a product
Satisfied customers create word-of-mouth referrals and repeat purchases
It occurs before the consumer makes a purchase decision
It is only relevant for luxury goods
questions.answer: Satisfied customers create word-of-mouth referrals and repeat purchases
questions.explanation: Post-purchase satisfaction drives customer loyalty and word-of-mouth, while dissatisfaction leads to returns and negative reviews — both critical to brand reputation.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Behavioral segmentation divides markets based on:" is: Purchase behavior, usage rate, and brand loyalty.
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Purchase behavior, usage rate, and brand loyalty is correct.

questions.question: In the introduction stage of the product life cycle, profits are typically:
options: Negative or very low due to high launch costs
questions.answer: Negative or very low due to high launch costs
questions.explanation: During introduction, sales are low and marketing/distribution costs are high, typically resulting in losses or minimal profit.

questions.question: A company prices a new product at $199 to signal quality while remaining just below $200. This is an example of:
questions.answer: Psychological pricing
questions.explanation: Pricing at $199 uses the just-below effect — consumers perceive it as significantly less than $200, while the .99 ending can also signal a deal.

questions.question: Exclusive distribution (like a Rolex boutique) is most appropriate for:
questions.answer: Luxury or specialty goods
questions.explanation: Exclusive distribution limits a product to a very small number of prestigious outlets, reinforcing the brand's luxury image and exclusivity.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which promotional element is most effective for complex, high-value B2B sales" is: Mass advertising.
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Personal selling.

questions.question: Which metric measures the percentage of website visitors who take a desired action?
questions.answer: Conversion rate
questions.explanation: Conversion rate = (conversions / total visitors) × 100. It measures the effectiveness of a website or campaign in achieving its goal (purchase, sign-up, etc.).

questions.question: The primary purpose of sales promotions is to:
options: Generate immediate consumer action or purchase
Replace advertising in the promotional mix
questions.answer: Generate immediate consumer action or purchase
questions.explanation: Sales promotions use short-term incentives (discounts, coupons, contests) to stimulate quick buying decisions — complementing the longer-term brand-building of advertising.

questions.question: A SWOT analysis identifies Opportunities as:
options: External factors the company can exploit to its advantage
questions.answer: External factors the company can exploit to its advantage
questions.explanation: Opportunities in SWOT are external — trends, market gaps, regulatory changes, or competitor weaknesses that the company can capitalize on.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Brand loyalty is valuable to a company because loyal customers:" is: Are less price-sensitive and generate repeat revenue.
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Are less price-sensitive and generate repeat revenue is correct.

questions.question: The 'augmented product' level includes:
options: The core benefit the consumer buys
Additional features like warranty, delivery, and after-sale service
questions.answer: Additional features like warranty, delivery, and after-sale service
questions.explanation: The augmented product goes beyond the physical product to include extra services and benefits — delivery, installation, warranty, and customer support — that differentiate offerings.

questions.question: Geographic segmentation divides markets by:
options: Location such as country, region, city, or climate
questions.answer: Location such as country, region, city, or climate
questions.explanation: Geographic segmentation recognizes that customer needs, preferences, and behaviors often vary by location — from country-level cultural differences to local climate needs.

questions.question: A pull promotional strategy aims to:
options: Push products through wholesalers to retailers
Create consumer demand that pulls products through the distribution channel
Reduce the number of distribution intermediaries
questions.answer: Create consumer demand that pulls products through the distribution channel
questions.explanation: A pull strategy targets end consumers directly with advertising and promotions, creating demand that causes retailers to stock the product in response to consumer requests.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following best describes relationship marketing" is: Focusing exclusively on making single transactions.
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Building long-term mutually beneficial relationships with customers.

questions.question: Which digital marketing channel involves earning placement in search results without paying per click?
questions.answer: Search Engine Optimization (SEO)
questions.explanation: SEO improves a website's organic (free) rankings in search results through relevant content, site structure, and backlinks — distinct from paid search advertising.

questions.question: Which segmentation strategy is likely used by Netflix to recommend content?
options: Behavioral and psychographic segmentation using viewing data
questions.answer: Behavioral and psychographic segmentation using viewing data
questions.explanation: Netflix uses algorithms analyzing viewing history, ratings, and preferences (behavioral and psychographic data) to personalize recommendations for each subscriber.

questions.question: When a cereal brand introduces a new granola bar product, this is an example of:
questions.answer: Brand extension
questions.explanation: Brand extension uses an existing brand name in a new product category — leveraging established brand recognition while diversifying the product portfolio.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following is considered unethical targeting in marketing" is: Targeting elderly consumers with misleading high-pressure timeshare sales tactics.
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Targeting elderly consumers with misleading high-pressure timeshare sales tactics is correct.

questions.question: The marketing concept holds that firms should:
options: Produce as much as possible to lower unit costs
Focus on making sales regardless of customer needs
Identify and satisfy customer needs more effectively than competitors
Maximize short-term profit above all else
questions.answer: Identify and satisfy customer needs more effectively than competitors
questions.explanation: The marketing concept states that organizational success comes from determining the needs of target markets and delivering desired satisfactions better than competitors.

questions.question: A company with a high NPS (Net Promoter Score) likely has:
options: A large number of loyal customers who recommend the brand to others
questions.answer: A large number of loyal customers who recommend the brand to others
questions.explanation: A high NPS indicates more promoters (9–10 ratings) than detractors (0–6) — customers who actively recommend the brand, driving organic growth.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a push promotional strategy?
options: A consumer campaign asking fans to share a hashtag
A TV commercial targeting end consumers
A trade promotion offering retailers discounts for prominently displaying the product
questions.answer: A trade promotion offering retailers discounts for prominently displaying the product
questions.explanation: Push strategies direct promotions at channel intermediaries (retailers, wholesalers) to encourage them to stock and promote the product — 'pushing' it toward consumers.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "When a store like Walmart sells products under its own brand name (e.g., Great Value), this is called:" is: Co-branding.
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Private label branding.

questions.question: Content marketing primarily generates value by:
options: Providing useful, relevant information that attracts and engages the target audience
questions.answer: Providing useful, relevant information that attracts and engages the target audience
questions.explanation: Content marketing builds trust and brand authority by offering genuinely useful content — rather than selling directly — establishing the brand as a knowledgeable resource.

questions.question: A marketing channel that goes from manufacturer → wholesaler → retailer → consumer is called:
questions.answer: A two-level channel
questions.explanation: Channel levels count the number of intermediaries. Manufacturer → Wholesaler → Retailer → Consumer has two intermediaries (wholesaler and retailer), making it a two-level channel.

questions.question: The decline stage of the product life cycle is characterized by:
options: Rapidly increasing sales and new competitors
Falling sales, shrinking market, and possible product discontinuation
questions.answer: Falling sales, shrinking market, and possible product discontinuation
questions.explanation: In the decline stage, sales fall due to changing consumer preferences, technology shifts, or new alternatives — firms must decide whether to harvest, divest, or rejuvenate the product.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Cost-plus pricing guarantees coverage of costs but may fail because it:" is: Ignores what customers value and what competitors charge.
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Ignores what customers value and what competitors charge is correct.

questions.question: Greenwashing is problematic because it:
options: Makes products more expensive to manufacture
Misleads consumers who want to make environmentally responsible choices
questions.answer: Misleads consumers who want to make environmentally responsible choices
questions.explanation: Greenwashing exploits consumers' environmental values with false or exaggerated eco-friendly claims, eroding trust and undermining genuine sustainability efforts.

questions.question: Which of the following is the BEST example of a SMART marketing objective?
options: Grow Instagram followers by 20% within 3 months through a daily posting schedule
questions.answer: Grow Instagram followers by 20% within 3 months through a daily posting schedule
questions.explanation: This objective is Specific (Instagram followers), Measurable (20%), Achievable (daily posts), Relevant (social media growth), and Time-bound (3 months).

questions.question: Market penetration as a growth strategy involves:
options: Developing new products for existing markets
Selling more of existing products to existing customers
questions.answer: Selling more of existing products to existing customers
questions.explanation: Market penetration (from Ansoff's Matrix) aims to grow by increasing market share — selling more of what you already have to customers you already serve.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which consumer buying decision involves the LEAST deliberation" is: Buying a house.
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Grabbing a candy bar at checkout.

questions.question: The purpose of competitive analysis in a marketing plan is to:
options: Understand rival firms' strengths, weaknesses, and strategies to find competitive advantage
questions.answer: Understand rival firms' strengths, weaknesses, and strategies to find competitive advantage
questions.explanation: Competitive analysis informs positioning and strategy by revealing where rivals are strong or weak, what they offer, and where market opportunities exist.

questions.question: Social proof in marketing (testimonials, user reviews) works by:
options: Leveraging other consumers' positive experiences to reduce risk and build trust
questions.answer: Leveraging other consumers' positive experiences to reduce risk and build trust
questions.explanation: Social proof reduces purchase risk by showing prospects that others like them have bought and been satisfied — one of the most powerful forms of persuasion in marketing.

questions.question: A brand's positioning is most effective when it is:
options: Relevant to the target segment, distinctive from competitors, and credibly delivered
questions.answer: Relevant to the target segment, distinctive from competitors, and credibly delivered
questions.explanation: Effective positioning occupies a unique, valued, and believable place in the minds of target customers — differentiating the brand in a way that drives preference and loyalty.
